title: Error Codes
text:
Error responses MUST use one of the following error codes in the `error.code` field.

Codes MUST be serialized as snake_case strings.

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `invalid_request` | The request JSON was malformed or the protocol version is unsupported. |
| `method_not_found` | The `method` field does not match any known RPC method. |
| `invalid_params` | The `params` field is missing required parameters or contains invalid values. |
| `no_match` | A pane selector resolved to zero matching panes. |
| `ambiguous_match` | A pane selector resolved to multiple panes and the `all` flag was not set. |
| `internal` | An unexpected internal error occurred in the plugin. |

Implementations MUST only use error codes defined in this clause.

The `error.message` field MUST contain a human-readable description of the error.

Rationale: A fixed set of error codes allows clients to handle errors programmatically without parsing human-readable messages. The snake_case serialization matches Rust's serde conventions and is unambiguous in JSON. The `no_match` and `ambiguous_match` codes are specific to the selector-based addressing model (per [[ADR-0004]]) and enable agents to distinguish "wrong selector" from "wrong parameters" from "internal failure."
